summary: Research within this cluster centered on the antiviral potential of phlorotannins derived from the edible brown alga *Eisenia bicyclis*, specifically targeting the murine norovirus (MNV). The compounds dieckol and phlorofucofuroeckol-A were identified as the primary active agents, with phlorofucofuroeckol-A demonstrating superior efficacy and a higher selective index (668.87) compared to dieckol (550.60), based on a 50% effective concentration (EC50) of 0.9 μM. The findings collectively highlighted the species *E. bicyclis* as a promising natural source for antiviral agents, though the geographic or ecological scope of the research was not explicitly addressed in the corpus.
